verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 (Cysteine-Rich Secretory Protein 1, also known as AEG1/DE/CRISP-1) is a secreted protein in the CRISP family, predominantly expressed in the epididymis and secreted into the epididymal lumen where it associates with sperm during maturation. It has no TM domain and is technically a secreted protein. However, CRISP1 is well-documented to bind stably to the sperm surface — both to the sperm head (decapacitation-related binding) and the tail — in a wash-resistant manner that persists through fertilization. This stable surface association with sperm is a hallmark of CRISP1 biology; it is not in free equilibrium with a soluble pool when on the sperm surface. This represents narrow tissue/cell-type-restricted surface display (sperm/male germline) qualifying as tissue_restricted_surface. No TM domain exists, and surface association is restricted to the germline/reproductive lineage context.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Whether CRISP1 sperm-surface binding is truly wash-resistant (stable_surface_attachment) vs. contextual/tissue-restricted; also unclear if any somatic cancer ecto-pool is documented.
